Container.Name: keyanu
Container.Overview:
Keyanu is a self-hosted infrastructure credential manager for homelabs and system administrators.
This single container includes the web UI, API, SQLite database and Nginx reverse proxy. No additional containers, Docker networks or manual configuration are required.
Network The default Docker bridge network is recommended for most installations.
Before starting the container, generate TWO different cryptographic keys.
SECRET_KEY Protects sessions, authentication and internal application security.
Generate: openssl rand -hex 32
ENCRYPTION_KEY Encrypts all stored passwords, SSH keys, API tokens, certificates, TOTP secrets and encrypted backups.
Generate: openssl rand -hex 32
IMPORTANT
• SECRET_KEY and ENCRYPTION_KEY must be different values. • Store both keys in a safe place before adding any data. • Keep a backup of both keys outside of your Keyanu appdata backups. • Never change or lose ENCRYPTION_KEY after credentials have been created. Existing encrypted data cannot be recovered without it.
On first boot, the initial administrator account is created from ADMIN_USERNAME and ADMIN_PASSWORD. You will be required to change the password after the first login.
Install Keyanu on Unraid with this XML template. Docker Hub Search install is not supported until Community Applications submission is completed because Docker Hub automatic conversion does not expose the correct Keyanu variables.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port used by the Keyanu web interface.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Persistent application data including the database, encrypted secrets, uploaded files and backups. Back up this folder regularly.

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Required. Protects sessions, authentication and internal application security. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Use a different value than ENCRYPTION_KEY. Store this key safely and keep a backup outside appdata. Leave the value unchanged when editing or updating the container.

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
Config.Description: Required. Encrypts passwords, SSH keys, API tokens, certificates, TOTP secrets and encrypted backups. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Use a different value than SECRET_KEY. Back up this key outside appdata. CRITICAL: never change or lose ENCRYPTION_KEY after data exists; existing encrypted data and 2FA secrets cannot be decrypted without the original value. Leave the value unchanged when editing or updating the container.

Config.Name: ADMIN_USERNAME
Config.Description: Initial administrator username. Existing user-entered values are preserved when editing or updating the container.

Config.Name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Temporary administrator password. You will be prompted to change it after first login. Existing user-entered values are preserved when editing or updating the container.

Config.Name: DATA_DIR
Config.Description: Internal application data directory. Leave unchanged unless you also change the Appdata path.

Config.Name: DEFAULT_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES
Config.Description: Default idle session timeout in minutes (720 = 12 hours).
